title: Nototriche orbignyana
intro: Nototriche orbignyana, commonly known as Chilean creeping thistle or prickly lettuce, is a flowering plant in the family Malvaceae. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, with reported benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and relief from anxiety and insomnia due to its sedative properties. The bioactive constituents of Nototriche orbignyana include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to alleviate stress and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Nototriche orbignyana is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, commonly known as the hibiscus or mallow family. It is native to South America and belongs to the genus Nototriche, which consists of various species of shrubs and small trees. Nototriche orbignyana is further classified within the tribe Malveae, a group of plants known for their showy flowers and fibrous leaves. The exact classification of Nototriche orbignyana can be further broken down into its rank and order, but it is generally accepted as a member of the Malvales order. At the highest taxonomic level, Nototriche orbignyana belongs to the kingdom Plantae.
